generated_oml:
prefix "DTDFVocab": https://bentleyjoakes.github.io/DTDF/vocab/DTDFVocab#
prefix "base": https://bentleyjoakes.github.io/base#

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance PositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder state variable retrieved from the crane controller bus via PhysicalCraneInterface"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CranePositionTelemetry
]

instance VelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Velocity measurement obtained from the crane controller bus via PhysicalCraneInterface"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneVelocityTelemetry
]

instance LoadCellSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Load cell state variable retrieved from the crane controller bus via PhysicalCraneInterface"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneLoadTelemetry
]

instance ActuatorStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator status flag retrieved from the crane controller bus via PhysicalCraneInterface"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneActuatorStatusTelemetry
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance CranePositionTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionSensor
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData
]

instance CraneVelocityTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VelocitySensor
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneVelocityData
]

instance CraneLoadTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LoadCellSensor
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneLoadData
]

instance CraneActuatorStatusTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ActuatorStatusSensor
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneActuatorStatusData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CranePositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑stamped position telemetry stored in TimescaleDB via PostgreSQL protocol"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneDataLogger
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneTimescaleDB
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionTelemetry
]

instance CraneVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑stamped velocity telemetry stored in TimescaleDB via PostgreSQL protocol"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneDataLogger
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneTimescaleDB
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneVelocityTelemetry
]

instance CraneLoadData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑stamped load telemetry stored in TimescaleDB via PostgreSQL protocol"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneDataLogger
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneTimescaleDB
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneLoadTelemetry
]

instance CraneActuatorStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑stamped actuator status telemetry stored in TimescaleDB via PostgreSQL protocol"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneDataLogger
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneTimescaleDB
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneActuatorStatusTelemetry
]

// Models
instance CraneKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Python‑encoded kinematic model of the gantry crane that computes motion trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneSimulationEngine
]

instance CraneTrajectoryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model for generating optimal control trajectories for the crane’s motion"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneSimulationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance CraneSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python simulation engine in a Docker container orchestrated by CI/CD pipelines"
    DTDFVocab:enables CraneTrajectoryControlService
    DTDFVocab:enables CraneValidationService
]

instance CraneDataLogger : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Logging application that writes telemetry into TimescaleDB via PostgreSQL protocol"
    DTDFVocab:enables CraneDataLoggingService
]

instance CraneTimescaleDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB time‑series database instance invoked by PostgreSQL protocol for persistent storage"
    DTDFVocab:enables CraneDataLoggingService
]

instance CraneMQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker for real‑time distribution of telemetry to downstream services"
    DTDFVocab:enables CraneValidationService
]

instance CraneGrafanaDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Grafana instance rendering dashboards for crane states and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables CraneDashboardService
]

// C6: Services
instance CraneTrajectoryControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating optimal motion trajectories derived from the crane’s kinematic model"
    DTDFVocab:provides GenerateOptimalTrajectoryAction
]

instance CraneDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that logs continuous real‑time telemetry of the physical crane for historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataRepositoryInsight
]

instance CraneDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service rendering data visualization dashboards rendering the crane’s state and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceMetricsInsight
]

instance CraneValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating live sensor data against model predictions to detect deviations and faults"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlDeviationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance GenerateOptimalTrajectoryAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action that computes and dispatches optimal trajectory commands to the crane’s controller"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HistoricalDataRepositoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight that provides access to stored historical telemetry for retrospective analysis"
]

instance PerformanceMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing metrics on payload throughput, energy consumption, and motion efficiency"
]

instance ControlDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight identifying deviations between live telemetry and model predictions indicating potential faults"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Control Application generates optimal movement trajectories through the Trajectory Generator Service and publishes them to a designated MQTT topic. The Physical Crane Interface subscribes to that topic, converts the high‑level trajectory commands into low‑level motor instructions, and forwards them to the crane controllers over the controller’s native bus. After each command execution the Physical Crane Interface publishes the actual actuator state back to the broker. The Continuous Validation Service consumes these measurements, compares them to the corresponding simulated trajectory, calculates a distance metric, and if the deviation exceeds a configured threshold, triggers a notification to the operator via the Grafana dashboard. Command acknowledgements are transmitted back through MQTT and logged in the database."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twin is designed for continuous, near‑real‑time operation: telemetry is ingested from the physical crane interface, transmitted via an MQTT broker to the twin, and compared against expected ranges in real time. No explicit periodic sampling frequency or latency bound is specified in the documentation; instead, the system processes data event‑driven as it arrives, thereby providing a dynamic, continuous time scale rather than a fixed update interval."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a component‑oriented software architecture composed of four core Python services: Telemetry (collecting sensor outputs from the physical crane via MQTT), Command (sending motor commands), Logging (writing captured states to a TimescaleDB database), and Validation (continuously comparing recorded state against expected trajectory). The services interconnect over an MQTT broker running on the same PC, and all persistence is handled by a TimescaleDB Post‑gres instance. The system is deployed directly on a personal computer without containerization or virtualization; it relies on open‑source tools such as Python, MQTT, TimescaleDB, and Grafana for visualization."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "All components are deployed on a single personal computer using Docker containers that communicate over a local Docker bridge network. The TimescaleDB service uses the PostgreSQL protocol on port 5432, the MQTT broker listens on port 1883 for intra‑service signaling, and Grafana serves the dashboard over HTTP port 3000. The Physical Crane Interface connects directly to the crane controller via its native serial/ethernet interface. The topology is star‑shaped around the MQTT broker, ensuring low‑latency (well below 10 ms on a local host) and high reliability through Docker’s container isolation. Optional redundancy can be achieved by replicating the database or MQTT broker containers as needed."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The full digital twin stack is hosted on an on‑premises edge node – a single personal computer. The stack is containerised with Docker: the TimescaleDB database, the Grafana dashboard, and the MQTT broker run as dedicated containers, while the Python services (Control Application, Logging Application, Trajectory Generator, Simulation Service, Continuous Validation Service) each run in their own containers on the same Docker network. This single‑node, “edge‑first” deployment eliminates dependence on external cloud providers. Computational requirements are satisfied by a typical workstation capable of running Docker and Python; storage is allocated to the TimescaleDB container for time‑series data. The architecture supports horizontal scaling by adding additional instances of any container if required, while ensuring all components remain co‑located on the same host for minimal latency and maximum control over the crane system."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The twin operates in a closed, local environment. All communication occurs over a local MQTT broker; there are no integrations with external digital twin frameworks, cloud services, or other enterprise systems. The design purposefully limits integration scope to local deployments, omitting any external data exchange protocols or interoperability standards beyond MQTT."
]
